question.text: Economic Value Added (EVA) is calculated by subtracting which of the following from net after-tax operating profit?

answer.text: Product of invested capital and percentage cost of capital

explanation.text: **The Logic:** EVA = Net after-tax operating profit - (Invested capital × Cost of capital). It measures true economic profit by accounting for the opportunity cost of capital employed. Positive EVA indicates the company is creating value above its cost of capital. **The Trap:** Some candidates confuse EVA with EBITDA or MVA. EVA specifically deducts the capital charge from operating profit. **Cross-Exam Context:** EVA and MVA are valuation concepts tested in X-A; similar value creation metrics appear in IX (Merchant Banking). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Companies consistently generating positive EVA are genuinely creating shareholder value — use this metric alongside P/E and P/B for comprehensive valuation.
